title: [CI:MACHINE] Podman5 qemu
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/machine-dev-5/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

***Important***: I understand this topic of PR is a priority.  But that must not become an excuse for careless merging.  This PR should **not** have been merged as-is, despite the failing secrets-scan check (details at the end).

[The `Total Success` task](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6050398692179968) is red for a very good reason.  It's spelled out [clearly in the failure message](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21326/checks?check_run_id=20778803475):

    Error: Risk of untested change merge.
    Please remove [CI:MACHINE] from title.

Merging `[CI:MACHINE]` PRs w/o a full CI runthrough seriously risks accepting a change that entirely breaks CI.  Worse, maintainers will be almost completely blind to this fact.  When's the last time you checked CI runs on a branch?

Everyone, please, slow down, take some breaths.  Sometimes we have workflows in place to save us from our selves.

---

Secondarily, the secret-scan failure should be considered advisory.  If it's a maintainer's PR, I don't care if it fails.  That check also sends mail to the podman-monitor list so it has built-in visibility.

If the secrets check is constantly failing, any maintainer is empowered to [follow the documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/.gitleaks/README.md#baseline) to update the baseline (in separate PR).  I promise it's really easy: download a file, append it into another file, commit then push.
